svg.text: Prompt Flow — From User Input to Agent Response
how the prompt is read, assembled, consumed, and returned at each pipeline stage
Prompt ASSEMBLED into full LLM input
PIPELINE — WorkItem passes through each stage in order
prompt is ONE of several context sources
WorkItem.context ← { prompt, messages, pipe_input, blackboard_snapshot, timestamp }
WorkItem.context ← { …, task_type, intent, entities } ← prompt is parsed but not yet sent anywhere
WorkItem.context ← { …, history[], memory_hits[] } ← prompt is a search key, not forwarded
If code refs found: symbols, file_paths added to context. This stage is a no-op when prompt has no code.
Match tools to task type + prompt
builds the tool list for the LLM
WorkItem.context.tools ← [web_search, web_fetch, mcp_tool] ← prompt is a selector, not yet sent
final string sent to the LLM.
the end as the user message.
"You are a research specialist. You have access to the following tools: web_search, web_fetch, mcp_tool. […]"
response: "I'll search for recent fusion energy research. [TOOL: web_search] query: fusion energy 2025"
results: [ "ITER achieves Q>1… Nature 2025… NIF records…" ]
final response text (no more actions → exits loop)
Retry policy: LLM failure triggers exponential backoff — 1 s, 2 s, 4 s — up to 3 attempts before agent_failed event.
strip markdown artifacts, normalize whitespace, enforce max_length
WorkItem.output ← formatted_response_text ← the original prompt is no longer referenced here
If invalid → re-enter REASON with updated context (up to 3 retries); assembled prompt rebuilt each time
emit(agent_finished, { agent_id, result, duration_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens })
EventBus → WebSocket /events → React UI (useEvents hook → TanStack Query invalidates cache → component re-renders)
am_future_wait() in Agent Server unblocks → HTTP 200 response also returned to original POST caller
Stages 1–5: prompt READ as raw string — used to build context, classify task, query memory, select tools. Never forwarded externally.
Stage 6: prompt ASSEMBLED — merged with system_prompt + history + tool_defs into one string. This is the actual LLM input.
Stage 7: assembled prompt SENT to LLM. Actions may cause re-assembly with tool results → LLM re-called. Original prompt is now deep in the message history.
